claim: Lee Cronin’s The Mummy landing in the $10m-$15m opening-weekend bracket is still more likely than not, but the swarm’s below-market view survives synthesis: the best independently checkable evidence supports low-teens plausibility, not the market’s 70% confidence in this narrow band. Post-synthesis, the most defensible range is 0.56 to 0.61, with the market likely overpricing bracket precision rather than obviously misreading direction.

frontmatter.contract_ambiguity_reason: exact 15m boundary resolves upward and finality may require confirmation
independently_verified_points: The Numbers is the governing settlement source for the final 3-day April 17-19 opening weekend figure
The film is opening wide domestically on April 17, 2026 via Warner Bros.
The Numbers context supports an approximately 3,200-theater launch
Accessible late tracking reports cluster around 14m-20m / 15m-20m rather than safely inside the bracket
The contract sends exact boundary hits to the higher bracket
frontmatter.verification_gap_summary: No directly audited primary trade tracking or preview data was available to pin the distribution tightly inside 10m-15m.
frontmatter.best_countercase_summary: A normal wide horror launch with recognizable IP could still settle neatly around 11m-14m, making the market directionally right.
frontmatter.main_reason_for_disagreement: personas mainly differed on how much to defer to the 70% market prior despite thin direct numeric evidence
frontmatter.resolution_mechanics_summary: Resolve on The Numbers final April 17-19 3-day opening weekend figure, with exact boundary values going to the higher bracket.
frontmatter.freshness_driver: Thursday previews and Friday daily gross can rapidly move the implied 3-day total across the 15m boundary
decision_blockers: No direct preview/Friday performance data yet
Independent numeric tracking remains thin relative to a narrow-band market
Exact boundary risk at 15m is materially hostile to this bracket
frontmatter.next_checkpoint: Thursday preview / Friday gross reporting before the weekend trajectory is fully repriced

sections.Alpha summary: Market implies 0.70; synthesized range is 0.56-0.61. That leaves a modest actionable lean against the current yes price, but not a huge one. The likely mispricing is overconfidence in a narrow middle bucket despite tracking and contract mechanics that leave meaningful risk of a just-over-15m miss.
sections.Input coverage: All five personas were available and usable. No persona was missing; all were medium-confidence and broadly coherent. I used the raw persona findings as canonical inputs and did a bounded synthesis-stage truth-finding pass using external checks on The Numbers, Box Office Theory, and a secondary tracking-summary article. Coverage is complete for synthesis purposes, but direct numeric sourcing remains thinner than ideal.
sections.Market-implied baseline: The synthesis baseline is the market’s 0.70 yes price. The swarm prior centered around 0.58, implying a substantial 12-point gap versus market and therefore a high verification bar before trusting a strong anti-market edge.
sections.Syndicated probability estimate: My final post-synthesis estimate is 0.56 to 0.61. The bracket remains the single most likely bucket, but the evidence still does not support a 70% confidence level because both the below-10m and above-15m tails remain live, and late accessible tracking leans toward the top edge rather than the center of the band.
sections.Difference from swarm-implied center: There is no major departure from the swarm-implied center of about 0.58. The truth-finding pass mostly validated the swarm’s main conclusion rather than overturning it: The Numbers and release-scale evidence support plausibility, while tracking summaries centered near 15m-20m prevent a move materially upward.
sections.Agreement or disagreement with market: I disagree with the market on confidence, not on basic direction. The market’s yes thesis is sensible because this is a wide Warner Bros. horror launch with recognizable IP and a plausible low-teens outcome. But 0.70 looks too rich for a pre-release range contract where the top boundary is hostile and the best public tracking cluster is not safely inside the band.
sections.Independent verification of edge: Verification quality is medium. I independently checked that The Numbers is the governing source, that the film is a wide April 17 release, that The Numbers context points to roughly 3,200 theaters, and that accessible outside tracking points around 14m-20m / 15m-20m. That is enough to support skepticism toward 70%, but not enough for high-confidence anti-market conviction because the most decisive data - previews, Friday actuals, or directly audited trade tracking - was still unavailable.
sections.Compression toward market due to verific: No meaningful compression toward the market was warranted beyond keeping the range tight around the swarm center. The synthesis-stage verification did not discover hidden evidence justifying 0.70; if anything, it modestly reinforced the under view because accessible tracking remained near or above the hostile top edge. I did not move further away from market because the same verification also confirmed structural plausibility for a low-teens opening.
sections.Timing and catalyst posture: The next key catalyst is Thursday previews and then Friday daily gross. That is the highest-information checkpoint because this contract is boundary-sensitive around 15m. The edge is likely to decay quickly once early grosses print, so waiting may improve accuracy but likely worsens any available pricing edge.
sections.Decision blockers: Main blockers are: no direct preview/Friday data yet; no fully audited primary trade tracking in hand; and a narrow-band contract with hostile treatment of exact 15m. There is still a usable synthesis despite those blockers, but they prevent high confidence.
sections.Implication for the question: The most likely interpretation remains that yes is slightly favored, but not at the market’s confidence level. Operationally, this means the contract should be treated as an overpriced narrow middle bucket rather than an obvious yes.
sections.Consensus across personas: All personas converged on several points: The Numbers is the governing source; the relevant window is the final April 17-19 3-day opening weekend; the film is opening wide; 10m-15m is plausible and may be the modal bracket; and 0.70 looks too high given thin direct numeric evidence and range fragility.
sections.Key disagreements across personas: Main disagreement was mixed but centered on weighting and market-pricing interpretation. The market-implied persona gave more credit to the 0.70 price as an information aggregator, landing at 0.62. Catalyst-hunter was most bearish at 0.55 because accessible tracking clustered at or above 15m and boundary rules are unfavorable. Base-rate emphasized ordinary wide-horror comps around 10m-12m but preserved a real Evil Dead Rise style upside tail. Variant-view and risk-manager stressed that narrow-range fragility itself is underpriced.
sections.Best countercase: Best countercase: the market may simply be right that a normal wide horror release with Mummy branding settles in the low teens often enough that 70% is not crazy, especially if hidden/private trade color is stronger than public evidence suggests. Market-implied and base-rate best represented this directionally yes countercase.
sections.Encapsulated assumptions: Shared assumptions: The Numbers final 3-day figure governs; the film opens wide on the correct weekend; wide horror/IP packaging makes low teens plausible. Contested assumptions: whether public tracking around 15m-20m is directionally strong enough to push the center above the bracket; whether the market embeds better unseen information; whether the realized theater count and walk-up demand more closely resemble ordinary 10m-12m horror comps or a stronger breakout case. Fragile assumptions: exact treatment of the top boundary in trader behavior, and whether previews materially skew the final 3-day total upward.
sections.Encapsulated evidence map: Strongest supporting evidence: wide April 17 release via Warner Bros.; The Numbers context indicating ~3,200 theaters; ordinary horror comps such as Night Swim and Abigail clustering near the target zone; recognizable horror/IP packaging. Strongest contradictory evidence: Evil Dead Rise style upside path; accessible tracking summaries citing 15m-20m and 14m-20m; exact boundary values resolve upward, making 15.0 a loser for this bracket. Governing evidence: contract text and The Numbers settlement mechanics. Ambiguous evidence: market price itself may reflect hidden information, but that was not independently verified.
sections.Evidence weighting: Most weight went to source-of-truth mechanics, release scale, and directly retrieved late tracking summaries. I downweighted same-ecosystem or secondhand tracking evidence because independence is limited, and I downweighted narrative hype/review chatter because it is less decision-useful than footprint plus numeric range evidence. I largely ignored generic franchise discourse that did not sharpen the opening-weekend distribution.
sections.Counterpoints / strongest disconfirming: The strongest disconfirming evidence against the below-market synthesis is that wide-release metadata, genre packaging, and some recent horror comps do make an 11m-14m outcome very believable. If the market has better private tracking than what was publicly accessible, then 0.70 could be closer to fair than this synthesis allows.
sections.Resolution or source-of-truth interpreta: This case is mostly clean on contract mechanics. Resolution should use The Numbers final 3-day opening-weekend figure for April 17-19, 2026. Studio estimates do not control. If finality is ambiguous, the market waits for confirmation from both The Numbers and Box Office Mojo. Exact boundary values resolve to the higher bracket, so a precise 15.0 is unfavorable to 10m-15m.
sections.Why this could create or destroy alpha: Alpha, if any, comes from the market overcompressing a plausible modal story into too much confidence for one narrow bucket. But alpha can be destroyed quickly if early grosses show the movie tracking comfortably to 11m-14m or if hidden trade information already underlies the market price. This is a calibration edge, not a dramatic thesis edge.
sections.What would falsify this interpretation /: A credible preview/Friday report implying a likely 11m-14m 3-day finish would move me toward the market. A strong early pace implying 16m+ would move me further below market. A direct primary trade forecast centered clearly below 15m would also strengthen the yes case. Any settlement clarification changing boundary handling would matter materially, though current rules look mostly clear.
sections.Highest-value next research: The single highest-value next check is Thursday preview and Friday gross reporting from credible box-office trade sources, because that will collapse the remaining uncertainty around the hostile 15m boundary.
sections.Source-quality assessment: Primary source class was strong: market rules plus The Numbers as governing resolution source. Most important secondary source class was trade-style tracking coverage, but direct auditability and independence were only medium to medium-low because public accessible reports were partly summarized through intermediaries or subscription-limited surfaces. Source-of-truth ambiguity is minor; the real bottleneck is thin independent numeric sourcing, not contract confusion.
sections.Verification impact: Yes, additional synthesis-stage verification was used. It materially reinforced the swarm’s caution: I found support for the core mechanics and release footprint, plus public tracking that leaned near or above the bracket top, but nothing strong enough to justify the market’s 70%. Cross-lane comparison also showed the sidecars were broadly faithful to the raw findings; no lane looked wildly distorted, though the market-implied lane was slightly more deferential to price than the independently verified evidence warranted.
sections.Persona contribution map: base-rate — supplied the most useful comp logic, especially the ordinary 10m-12m horror cluster and the Evil Dead Rise upside tail. catalyst-hunter — best highlighted the immediate catalysts, public tracking cluster near 15m+, and the practical importance of the hostile top boundary. market-implied — preserved the strongest case for respecting the market prior and not overstating anti-market confidence. risk-manager — best framed the call as confidence calibration rather than dramatic directional disagreement. variant-view — most clearly articulated narrow-range fragility and why modal plausibility does not equal 70% bracket probability.
sections.Reusable lesson signals: Durable lesson candidate: in opening-weekend range markets, release metadata and wide-footprint checks can establish plausibility, but edge often lives in boundary mechanics and unverified distribution width rather than the modal narrative. Underbuilt driver candidate: a reusable box-office range fragility / launch-scale driver may help. Source-quality lesson: public evidence can validate direction while remaining too weak to justify high-confidence narrow-band pricing. Reusability confidence: medium.
sections.Orchestrator review suggestions: review later for durable lesson: yes; review later for driver candidate: yes; review later for canon or linkage issue: yes; review later for swarm-method issue: yes. Reason: this case repeatedly exposed the same pattern — strong settlement mechanics, decent footprint context, but weak direct numeric independence for a narrow bracket market — which looks methodologically reusable.
sections.Recommended follow-up: Request decision-maker review with a modest below-market stance, then refresh on Thursday previews / Friday actuals before any final action. If early grosses arrive before decision, update immediately; otherwise carry forward as slight-yes directionally, but below-market on confidence.
